content.problemStatement:
**This problem has the same setup as the easy version. The difference is here you need to find the sum over all substrings instead of just the value of the string.**

You are the owner of a shop with NNN different unique items to sell, numbered 1,2,‚Ä¶,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,‚Ä¶,N. The items have been numbered such that item iii is always larger (in size) than item i‚ąí1i - 1i‚ąí1. The cost of the iii-th item is CiC_iCi‚Äč. The cost of an item **does not have** any correlation with the size.

There is a display zone in your shop, and customers are only able to see items that are on the display zone. The rules are as follows:


At the start of each day, you can choose to add some items to the display zone.
Once an item has been placed on the display zone, **it cannot be removed** until a customer buys it.
When a customer comes, he must see at least 222 different items on the display zone, to ensure that he has some freedom of choice.
Once a customer buys an item, it cannot be bought by another customer, and it cannot be kept on the display zone.
K(1‚Č§K<N)K (1 \le K < N)K(1‚Č§K<N) customers will come to your shop, one by one, on days 111, 222, ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶, KKK respectively. On the iii-th day, the customer has a preference of AiA_iAi‚Äč, where Ai=0A_i = 0Ai‚Äč=0 means that he will buy the smallest item (which is present on the display zone), and Ai=1A_i = 1Ai‚Äč=1 means that he will buy the largest item (which is present on the display zone). Note that AAA is a binary string.

You want to maximize the total revenue that you get. Your revenue is the sum of CiC_iCi‚Äč over all sold items.

Let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the maximum possible revenue corresponding to the binary string AAA of length KKK (representing the preferences of the customers).

You are given NNN, the cost of all items CiC_iCi‚Äč and a binary string AAA of length KKK. Find ‚ąĎL=1‚ą£A‚ą£‚ąĎR=L‚ą£A‚ą£f(A[L,R])\sum_{L = 1}^{|A|} \sum_{R = L}^{|A|} f(A[L, R])‚ąĎL=1‚ą£A‚ą£‚Äč‚ąĎR=L‚ą£A‚ą£‚Äčf(A[L,R]) modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7, where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] represents the string ALAL+1‚Ä¶ARA_L A_{L + 1} \ldots A_RAL‚ÄčAL+1‚Äč‚Ä¶AR‚Äč, i.e. you are required to find the sum over all substrings of AAA.

Note that in the instance of the problem f(A[L,R])f(A[L, R])f(A[L,R]), there are a total of R‚ąíL+1R - L + 1R‚ąíL+1 customers who come to your shop, and not KKK.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers NNN and KKK - the number of items, and the number of customers.
The second line contains NNN integers - C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč.
The third line contains a binary string AAA of length KKK.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line ‚ąĎL=1‚ą£A‚ą£‚ąĎR=L‚ą£A‚ą£f(A[L,R])\sum_{L = 1}^{|A|} \sum_{R = L}^{|A|} f(A[L, R])‚ąĎL=1‚ą£A‚ą£‚Äč‚ąĎR=L‚ą£A‚ą£‚Äčf(A[L,R]) modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There is only one substring, i.e. 111, and here f(1)=10f(1) = 10f(1)=10.
Test Case 2 : f(11)=9f(11) = 9f(11)=9 and f(1)=5f(1) = 5f(1)=5. Thus, the answer is 9+5+5=199 + 5 + 5 = 199+5+5=19.
Test Case 4 : Do not forget to print the answer modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
